context.description: The article detail page now includes tag-management and bookmark actions, but the bookmark CTA can be confused with the global Bookmarks navigation link. Action buttons also lacked a clear active visual state after interaction.

problem.summary: Article-detail actions are visually ambiguous and do not clearly show which action is active.

details.issue: The navbar Bookmarks link is a destination page, while the article-detail bookmark button is an action, but the wording made that distinction weak.
After interacting with detail actions, active state feedback was not strong enough to confirm selection.

problem.expected_behavior: The article-detail bookmark button should read like an action on the current article. Active actions on the detail page should visibly change color so the selected state is obvious.
problem.actual_behavior: Users can mistake the bookmark behavior for the global bookmarks list entry point, and detail action buttons remain too visually similar before and after interaction.

requirements.requirement: Use article-specific wording for the detail bookmark button
Keep navbar Bookmarks as a separate navigation affordance
Apply a clear active color to the selected action
Keep inactive actions visually quieter than the active one

acceptance_criteria.criteria: Bookmark action on article detail is no longer confused with the navbar Bookmarks link
Bookmarked state uses a distinct active visual style
Add Tag editing state uses a distinct active visual style
